2.q: overexpressed PeTCP10 was reported to enhance salt tolerance of transgenic plants through improving H2O2-scavenging capacity during the vegetative growth stage.

3.q: PeWRKY83 ... plays a positive role in salt tolerance by regulating stress-induced ABA synthesis; Overexpression plants accumulated more endogenous ABA under salt stress conditions.

4.q: protein interaction analysis revealed that PeSNAC-1 could interact with stress responsive PeSNAC-2/4 and PeNAP-1/4/5 in both yeast and plant cells

6.q: PeWRKY83 interacted with PeVQ14 in vivo by BiFC (bimolecular fluorescence complementation) assays in the leaves of tobacco, and the YFP signal was detected in the nuclear compartment of transformed cells.

7.q: Yeast two-hybrid and bimolecular fluorescence complementation analyses indicated that PeVQ28 and WRKY83 interactions occurred in the nucleus.

8.q: PeSAPK4 could interact with and phosphorylate PeMYB99 in vivo and in vitro, wherein they synergistically accelerated PeTIP4-3 transcription.

13.q: Yeast one-hybrid assays demonstrated that two PeABF1 proteins could bind to the promoter of PeDHN4, suggesting that PeDHN4 may regulate stress responses through the ABA signaling pathway.

14.q: PeMYB99 exhibited an ability to independently bind to and activate PeTIP4-3, which augmented tolerance to drought and salt stress.

15.q: PeSNAC-1 likely improved salt and drought stress tolerance via modulating gene regulation in both ABA-dependent and independent signaling pathways in transgenic rice.

16.q: These results suggest that PeVQ28 functions in the positive regulation of salt tolerance mediated by an ABA-dependent signaling pathway.

17.q: H2O2 and malondialdehyde (MDA) contents were significantly lower in PheTCP9 OE transgenic Arabidopsis than WT. Catalase (CAT) activity was also much improved in transgenic Arabidopsis than WT under salt conditions.

24.q: The results showed that there are two pairs of collinear genes (PeDHN8 and PeDHN4, PeDHN5 and PeDHN6) of PeDHNs in moso bamboo, which may be related to the two rounds of genome duplication events in moso bamboo.

27.q: the ectopic overexpression of PeAOX1b_2 in Arabidopsis enhanced seed germination and seedling establishment, increased the total respiratory rate and the proportion of AOX respiratory pathway in leaf

28.q: The cleavage of PeSNAC1 mRNA guided by ped-miR164b was validated using RLM-50 RACE.
